title: Led lighting system
abstract: An LED lamp arrangement (1) adapted for fitting in a luminaire. The luminaire is adapted for a fluorescent lamp and having a ballast. The LED lamp arrangement has a plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27 ) and two or more electrodes (22 ) for releasably connecting to the luminaire and for conducting current from the ballast for supply to the LEDs. The LED lamp arrangement further has a sensor circuit (21) and a protection circuit (24). The sensor circuit and the protection circuit are adapted to increase the total load impedance of the LED lamp arrangement if the ballast is a constant power electronic ballast.
claims:
Claims C LA I MS 1 . An LED lamp arrangement (1) adapted for fitting in a luminaire, the luminaire adapted for a fluorescent lamp and having a ballast, the LED lamp arrangement comprising: a plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27); more than two electrodes (22) for releasably connecting to the luminaire and for receiving a current from the ballast; one or more rectifier circuits for rectifying the current and for supplying a rectified current to the LEDs; a sensor circuit (21) adapted to measure one or more parameters related to an amount of current drawn from the ballast by the LED lamp arrangement; and a protection circuit (24) connected to receive the rectified current and adapted to control an amount of current drawn from the ballast by the LED lamp arrangement (1), wherein the LED lamp arrangement (1) is arranged to operate in a plurality of operation modes including a first operation mode and a second operation mode, wherein the LED lamp arrangement (1) has a different load impedance in the first operation mode then in the second operation mode, and wherein the protection circuit is arranged to automatically switch the LED lamp arrangement (1) from the fi st operation mode to the second operation mode, if the measured one or more parameters exceed a threshold which indicates whether the ballast is a constant current electronic ballast or not a constant current electronic ballast. 2. The LED lamp arrangement ( 1 ) of claim 1 , wherein in the first operation mode the protection circuit has a first impedance, and in the second operation mode the protection circuit has a second impedance which is different from the first impedance. 3. The LED lamp arrangement ( 1 ) of claim 1 or 2, wherein the sensor circuit ( 2 1 ) is adapted to measure the one or more parameters from the rectified current received from the one or more rectifier circuits. 4. The LED lamp arrangement (1) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sensor circuit (21) comprises a fuse ( 1 5, 1 5a), and the protection circuit (24) comprises an impedance ( 16, 1 6a, 1 6b) and the fuse ( 1 5, 1 5a), the impedance (16, 16a. 16b) being arranged 

 in series with the plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27), and the fuse (15, 1 5a) being arranged in parallel with the impedance, and wherein the first operation mode corresponds to a first state of the protection circuit (24) in which the impedance (16, 16a, 1 6b ) is shorted by the fuse ( 1 5, 1 5a ), and the second operation mode corresponds to a second state of the protection circuit (24) in which the fuse ( 1 5, 1 5a) is blown. 5. The LED lamp arrangement (1) according to claim 4, wherein the one or more rectifier circuits are arranged to output the rectified current having an AC part at a frequency higher than the frequency of the current received from the ballast, and wherein the impedance (16, 16a, 16b) has a total inductance value between 0. 12 mil and 0.3 mi l . 6. The LED lamp arrangement (1) according to claim 4 or 5, wherein the impedance comprises a transformer ( 16b) having a first w inding (16c) and a second w inding ( 16d ), wherein the fi st winding (16c) is arranged in series w ith the plural ity of LEDs (25, 26, 27), and the second winding ( 1 6d ) forms a closed loop with the fuse (15, 15a). 7. The LED lam arrangement (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 -6, wherein the measured one or more parameters represent an amount of current received from the ballast by the LED lamp arrangement (1) and/or an amount of voltage supplied by the ballast across the LED lamp arrangement (1). 8. The LED lamp arrangement (1) according to any one of claims I -7, wherein the plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27) are arranged in a plurality of groups; wherein the groups of LEDs are connectable in a plurality of circuit configurations including at least a first circuit configuration, and a second ci cuit configuration hav ing a different circuit arrangement of the groups of LEDs in which at least a portion of the groups of LEDs are connected to each other differently than in the first circuit configuration; wherein the first circuit configuration corresponds to the first operation mode, and the second ci cuit configuration corresponds to the second operation mode; and 

 wherein the LED lamp arrangement (1) is adapted to automatically switch a connection of the plurality of groups of LEDs from the first circuit configuration to the second ci cuit configuration. 9. The LED lamp arrangement (1) according to claim 8, wherein the second circuit configuration comprises a greater number of LEDs connected in a series string than the first circuit configuration. 10. The LED lamp arrangement (1) according to claim 8 or 9, wherein the LED lamp arrangement (1) is further arranged to automatically switch from the first operation mode or the second operation mode to a third operation mode, if the measured one or more parameters exceed a second threshold. 1 1 . The LED lamp arrangement (1) according to any one of claims 8-10, wherein the sensor circuit (21) comprises a fuse (15, 15a), and the protection circuit (24) comprises an impedance (16, 16a, 16b) and the fuse (15, 1 5a), the impedance (16, 16a, 16b) being arranged in series with the plural ity of LEDs (25, 26, 27), and the fuse (15, 1 5a) being arranged in parallel with the impedance, wherein the protection circuit (24) is arranged to automatically switch to a third operation mode if the measured one or more parameters exceed a second threshold, and wherein: in the first operation mode, the impedance (16, 16a, 16b) is shorted by the fuse ( 1 5, 15a), and the plural ity of groups of LEDs (25, 26, 27 ) are connected in the first circuit configuration; - in the second operation mode, the impedance (16, 1 6a, 16b) is shorted by the fuse ( 1 5, 15a), and the plural ity of groups of LEDs ( 25, 26, 27) are connected in the second circuit configuration; and in the third operation mode, the fuse ( 1 5, 1 5a) is blown, and the plural ity of groups of LEDs (25, 26, 27) are connected in the first circuit configuration or the second circuit configuration. 12. The LED lamp arrangement (1) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27) are arranged in a plural ity of groups, wherein the groups of LEDs are connect able in a plurality of circuit configurations, wherein at least a portion of the groups of LEDs are connected to each other differently in different ci cuit configurations, and wherein the LED lamp arrangement comprises a circuit for generating an output indicating w hether the ballast is a magnetic ballast or not a magnetic ballast, and a circuit for changing a connection of the plural ity of groups of LEDs between a circuit configuration to another circuit configuration on the basis of the output. 13. A method for operating an LED lamp arrangement (1) in a plural ity of operation modes, the LED lamp arrangement (1) comprising a plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27) and being fitted in a luminaire, the luminal re being adapted for a fluorescent lamp and having a ballast, the method comprising: conducting a current from the ballast to the LED lamp arrangement (1); - rectifying the current for supply to the plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27); measuring one or more parameters related to an amount of current drawn from the ballast by the LED lamp arrangement (1); conducting the rectified current to the plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27) and a protection ci cuit (24); and - increasing a load impedance of the LED lamp arrangement (1) by means of the protecting circuit ( 24 ) if the measured one or more parameters exceed a threshold w hich indicates whether the ballast is a constant current electronic ballast or not a constant current electronic ballast. 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein increasing the load impedance comprises increasing an inductance through w hich the current flows. 1 5. The method according to claim 1 or 14, w herein the plurality of LEDs (25, 26, 27) arc arranged in a plural ity of groups, w herein the groups of LEDs are connectable in a plu ality of circuit configurations including at least a fi st circuit configuration, and a second circuit configuration hav ing a different circuit arrangement of the groups of LEDs in which at 

 least a portion of the groups of LEDs are connected to each other differently than in the first circuit configuration; and wherein increasing the load impedance comprises switching from the first circuit configuration to second circuit configuration.
